content:
# Numbers Chapter 4 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 150-152 | Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0113-0114 (LXX)
**Source Images:** `NUM_004_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_004_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_004_page_003.jpg`; `NUM_004_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_004_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

**2** "Take a census of the sons of Kohath from among the sons of Levi, by their clans, by the houses of their fathers,

**3** from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, all who enter the service to do work in the tent of meeting.

**4** This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting: the most holy things.

**5** And when the camp sets out, Aaron and his sons shall come and take down the veil of the screen and cover the ark of the testimony with it.

**6** And they shall put on it a covering of tachash skin and shall spread over it a cloth wholly of blue, and shall set its poles in place.

**7** And over the table of the presence they shall spread a cloth of blue and put on it the dishes and the spoons and the bowls and the jars for the drink offering; and the continual bread shall be on it.

**8** And they shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet and shall cover it with a covering of tachash skin, and shall set its poles in place.

**9** And they shall take a cloth of blue and cover the lampstand for the light, and its lamps and its tongs and its firepans and all its oil vessels with which they minister to it.

**10** And they shall put it and all its vessels within a covering of tachash skin and shall put it on the carrying frame.

**11** And over the golden altar they shall spread a cloth of blue and cover it with a covering of tachash skin, and shall set its poles in place.

**12** And they shall take all the vessels of ministry with which they minister in the sanctuary and put them in a cloth of blue and cover them with a covering of tachash skin, and shall put them on the carrying frame.

**13** And they shall remove the ashes from the altar and spread a cloth of purple over it.

**14** And they shall put on it all its vessels with which they minister upon it: the firepans, the forks, the shovels, and the basins -- all the vessels of the altar -- and they shall spread on it a covering of tachash skin, and shall set its poles in place.

**15** And when Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the vessels of the sanctuary, as the camp sets out, after that the sons of Kohath shall come to carry them, but they shall not touch the holy things lest they die. These are the burden of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting.

**16** And the charge of Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall be the oil for the light and the fragrant incense and the continual grain offering and the anointing oil, the oversight of all the tabernacle and all that is in it, in the sanctuary and in its vessels."

**17** And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

**18** "Do not cut off the tribe of the clans of the Kohathites from among the Levites.

**19** But do this for them, that they may live and not die when they draw near to the most holy things: Aaron and his sons shall come and appoint each of them to his service and to his burden.

**20** But they shall not go in to look upon the holy things even for a moment, lest they die."

**21** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**22** "Take a census also of the sons of Gershon, by the houses of their fathers, by their clans.

**23** From thirty years old and upward to fifty years old you shall number them, all who enter to perform service, to do work in the tent of meeting.

**24** This is the service of the clans of the Gershonites, in serving and in carrying:

**25** They shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle and the tent of meeting, its covering and the covering of tachash skin that is on top of it, and the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting,

**26** and the hangings of the court and the screen for the entrance of the gate of the court that is around the tabernacle and around the altar, and their cords and all the vessels of their service; and all that is to be done for them, they shall perform.

**27** At the command of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service of the sons of the Gershonites, in all their burdens and in all their service; and you shall assign to them as a charge all their burdens.

**28** This is the service of the clans of the sons of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting, and their charge shall be under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

**29** As for the sons of Merari, you shall number them by their clans, by the houses of their fathers.

**30** From thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old you shall number them, everyone who enters the service to do the work of the tent of meeting.

**31** And this is the charge of their burden, according to all their service in the tent of meeting: the frames of the tabernacle and its bars and its pillars and its bases,

**32** and the pillars of the court all around and their bases and their pegs and their cords, with all their vessels and all their service; and by name you shall assign the vessels of the charge of their burden.

**33** This is the service of the clans of the sons of Merari, according to all their service in the tent of meeting, under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest."

**34** And Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation numbered the sons of the Kohathites by their clans and by the houses of their fathers,

**35** from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, everyone who entered the service for work in the tent of meeting.

**36** And their numbered ones by their clans were two thousand seven hundred and fifty.

**37** These are the numbered ones of the clans of the Kohathites, all who served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

**38** And the numbered ones of the sons of Gershon, by their clans and by the houses of their fathers,

**39** from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, everyone who entered the service for work in the tent of meeting --

**40** their numbered ones by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, were two thousand six hundred and thirty.

**41** These are the numbered ones of the clans of the sons of Gershon, all who served in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of YHWH.

**42** And the numbered ones of the clans of the sons of Merari, by their clans, by the houses of their fathers,

**43** from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, everyone who entered the service for work in the tent of meeting --

**44** their numbered ones by their clans were three thousand two hundred.

**45** These are the numbered ones of the clans of the sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

**46** All the numbered ones whom Moses and Aaron and the leaders of Israel numbered, of the Levites, by their clans and by the houses of their fathers,

**47** from thirty years old and upward even to fifty years old, everyone who entered to do the work of service and the work of carrying in the tent of meeting --

**48** their numbered ones were eight thousand five hundred and eighty.

**49** According to the commandment of YHWH they were appointed by the hand of Moses, each man to his service and to his burden; and they were numbered, as YHWH commanded Moses.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Textual Notes
1. **v.6 -- "tachash skin"** (Heb. *'or tachash*, עוֹר תָּחַשׁ): The identification of the *tachash* remains uncertain. The MT consistently uses this term throughout the chapter (vv.6, 8, 10, 11, 12, 14, 25). The LXX (Vaticanus) renders it as *derma hyakinthinon* (skin of hyacinth/blue), interpreting it as a color rather than an animal. Other proposals include "dolphin skin," "dugong skin," or "fine leather." We retain the transliteration to preserve the ambiguity of the Hebrew.
2. **v.7 -- "table of the presence"** (Heb. *shulchan hapanim*, שֻׁלְחַן הַפָּנִים): Lit. "table of the face/faces." Rendered formally as "table of the presence" to preserve the theological weight of the divine presence implied by *panim*.
3. **v.7 -- "continual bread"** (Heb. *lechem hatamid*, לֶחֶם הַתָּמִיד): The MT reads literally "the bread of continuity." The LXX reads *artous tous diapantos* ("the continual loaves").
4. **v.13 -- "remove the ashes"** (Heb. *vidishnu*, וְדִשְּׁנוּ): The piel of *d-sh-n* can mean "to remove the fat-ashes" or "to cleanse of ashes." The altar in question is the bronze altar of burnt offering.
5. **v.15 -- "but they shall not touch"** (Heb. *velo yig'u*, וְלֹא־יִגְּעוּ): The prohibition against touching the holy objects is absolute. The LXX (Vaticanus) confirms this reading with *ou hapsontai*.
6. **v.20 -- "even for a moment"** (Heb. *kevalah*, כְּבַלַּע): This rare word has been variously interpreted. Lit. "as a swallowing," hence "an instant" or "a glance." The LXX reads *exapina* ("suddenly"). We render it temporally: "even for a moment," capturing the sense that even the briefest exposure to the uncovered holy objects brings death.

### Structural Notes
7. **Three-part division**: The chapter follows a clear tripartite structure: (a) duties of the Kohathites (vv.1-20), (b) duties of the Gershonites (vv.21-28), (c) duties of the Merarites (vv.29-33), followed by the census results (vv.34-49).
8. **Kohathite prominence**: The Kohathites receive the longest and most detailed instructions because they carry the most sacred objects (the ark, table, lampstand, altars). Their section alone includes the stern warning against looking upon or touching the holy things.
9. **Chain of authority**: The text establishes a clear hierarchy -- YHWH commands Moses and Aaron (v.1), Aaron and his sons prepare the sacred objects (vv.5-15), the Kohathites carry them (v.15), while the Gershonites and Merarites serve under the direction of Ithamar (vv.28, 33).

### Cross-References
10. **v.3 -- "thirty years old"**: This age requirement differs from the twenty-five years in Num 8:24. The rabbinical tradition reconciles this by positing a five-year apprenticeship before full service. The fifty-year upper limit appears also in Num 8:25.
11. **v.5 -- "the veil of the screen"** (Heb. *parokhet hamasak*): This is the inner curtain separating the Holy of Holies from the Holy Place (cf. Exod 26:31-33; 36:35).
12. **v.15 -- "lest they die"**: The gravity of unauthorized contact with holy objects is attested in 2 Sam 6:6-7 (Uzzah) and Num 1:51.

### LXX Variants (Vaticanus)
13. **v.3**: The LXX adds specification of those "who come to do works" (*ergadzesthai erga*), closely matching the MT *latsavo tsava la'asot melakha*.
14. **v.14**: The LXX lists the altar vessels in the same order as the MT, confirming textual stability for this inventory.
15. **v.32**: The LXX renders "by name you shall assign" as *kat' onoma epiksepsasthe*, preserving the MT's emphasis on individual accountability for each assigned item.
16. **v.48**: The total of eight thousand five hundred and eighty is consistent across both MT and LXX traditions, confirming the arithmetic: 2,750 (Kohathites) + 2,630 (Gershonites) + 3,200 (Merarites) = 8,580.
